comments.body:
> But if you were to put classes in both places, we wouldn't be able to merge the classes, do you think it matters 🤔

Why not? Radix merges classes when using `asChild` and even event handlers. I'd expect the same on all components

The short form `render={<MyComponent />}` will merge everything! But having finer control with the long form also means you have ensure you don't overwrite the render props yourself.
(I suppose the render prop would be explained in more detail, probably in [composition](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/1007)?)

Oh 🤦‍♂️
I'm just realising my mistake. Of course it makes sense, we don't merge things in the long form.

Dismiss the issue then

**This issue has been closed.** If you have a similar problem but not exactly the same, please open a [new issue](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/new/choose).
Now, if you have additional information related to this issue or things that could help future readers, feel free to leave a comment.
No worries, still found a minor bug from this ~

**This issue has been closed.** If you have a similar problem but not exactly the same, please open a [new issue](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/new/choose).
Now, if you have additional information related to this issue or things that could help future readers, feel free to leave a comment.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: [all components] Render function props override `className`
